# @json-express/api-graphql — AI Context

GraphQL API generator — replaces REST with a GraphQL schema generated from your models.

## Install — that is the whole integration
```
npm i @json-express/api-graphql
```
Discovery is automatic; if `api-rest` is also installed choose with `jex.api=@json-express/api-graphql`. **Never import or register this in code.**

## Configuration (.env — case-insensitive)
| Key | Default | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `jex.api.graphql.endpoint` | `/graphql` | Mount path (POST executes queries) |
| `jex.api.graphql.graphiql` | enabled in development | Serve the GraphiQL IDE on GET |
| `jex.api.graphql.assetsBaseUrl` | `https://unpkg.com` | Mirror base for GraphiQL/React assets (airgapped deployments) |
| `jex.auth.secret` / `jex.auth.jwksUri` (+ `audience`, `issuer`, `algorithms`) | (none) | Shared `auth.*` namespace — bearer tokens are verified and exposed to resolvers/access rules |

## What it unlocks (all driven from `models/`)
- Queries (`albums`, `album(id)`), mutations (`createAlbum`, …) generated per collection from model fields.
- Model `access` blocks enforced inside resolvers; errors map to structured GraphQL errors (NOT_FOUND, FORBIDDEN, …).
- Custom GraphQL extensions declared in models — never hand-written SDL files.
